outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Iranian source says US initiated 'outreach' to end war, as Israel and Tehran continue to trade strikes
outlets.summary: CNN reports an Iranian source says the US initiated back-channel contact to end the war, even as Israel bombed Isfahan and Hezbollah killed a woman in northern Israel on Day 25.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Trump says Iran is eager for a deal to end the war as he extends deadline to allow for diplomacy
outlets.summary: WaPo frames Trump's 5-day pause on power plant strikes as a de-escalation, noting Iran's private signals of deal readiness versus its public denials.

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: Iran defiant as Trump delays striking power plants for 5 days
outlets.summary: NPR reports on the sharp contradiction between Trump's claim of 'productive talks' and Iran's immediate categorical denial — with Araghchi calling the claim designed to 'mislead oil traders.'

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Iran war live updates: Trump says war has been won, announces 5-day pause on power plant strikes as Iran denies talks
outlets.summary: CNN covers Trump's dramatic "war has been won" announcement and the 5-day pause on Iranian power plant strikes, while reporting Iran's denials of direct negotiations. Coverage emphasizes confusion and mixed signals amid ongoing strikes.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump announces 'productive' Iran talks to end conflict; Israel, Iran continue to trade strikes
outlets.summary: Fox leads with Trump's claim of productive peace talks, while also covering the ongoing military strikes on both sides.

outlets.name: Breitbart
outlets.headline: Trump's ever-shifting positions on the war with Iran
outlets.summary: Breitbart tracks Trump's evolving public stance on the Iran conflict, from launching the strikes to now pausing and claiming peace talks.

outlets.name: Washington Examiner
outlets.headline: Trump postpones strikes on Iranian power plants for five days
outlets.summary: Washington Examiner reports the 5-day pause as a potential off-ramp to the war, with Trump framing it as momentum toward resolution while noting CENTCOM destroyed Qom drone engine plant.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump declares Iran war won, IDF destroys IRGC HQ as 82nd Airborne deploys to Middle East
outlets.summary: Fox News frames Trump's peace announcement positively as successful dealmaking, highlights IRGC HQ destruction and 82nd Airborne deployment as leverage, and downplays Iran's denials.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: US-Israel war on Iran: What's happening on Day 25 of attacks?
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera's Day 25 explainer covers the Habbaniyah PMF strike, Hezbollah's killing of an Israeli woman, Iran's cluster warhead use, and Pakistan's offer to host US-Iran talks.

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: Pakistan is poised to host the U.S. and Iran for talks to end the war
outlets.summary: NPR reports that Pakistan has formally offered Islamabad as a neutral venue for US-Iran talks, with two meeting formats being discussed including one involving Araghchi and Witkoff.

outlets.name: Euronews
outlets.headline: Iran threatens to destroy regional infrastructure as Israel warns war to last weeks
outlets.summary: Euronews leads with Iran's Defense Council threat to mine the Persian Gulf and strike US-allied power plants across the Gulf if attacked, alongside Trump's 5-day pause announcement.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Despite Trump's peace talk claims, US-Israeli attacks continue to hit Iran on Day 25
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera highlights contradiction between Trump's "war has been won" claims and continued large-scale strikes including IRGC HQ destruction and Bushehr proximity attack. Emphasizes Iran's denials and civilian suffering.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Woman killed, 2 others injured in Hezbollah rocket attack in northern Israel — Day 25 liveblog
outlets.summary: ToI's liveblog covers Hezbollah rocket killing a woman at Mahanayim Junction, IDF airstrike on Isfahan's explosives factory, and Iran's cluster warhead strike on Petah Tikva.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: What Are We Waiting For? — Israel at Day 25 of the Iran War
outlets.summary: Haaretz opinion questions the war's trajectory as civilian deaths mount, Mossad's predicted uprising fails to materialize, and Iran continues to fire cluster warheads at Israeli cities.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Trump: US Holding Talks With Iran Over 'Complete' Resolution of Mideast Hostilities
outlets.summary: Haaretz Day 24 coverage leads with Trump's claim of productive Iran talks — noting that Iran immediately denied any contact and that IDF delivered 'unprecedented' wave of Tehran strikes in the same morning.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Trump says talks with Iran progressing; Israel said to fear premature ceasefire that falls short of war goals
outlets.summary: Times of Israel focuses on Israeli anxiety over a premature ceasefire. Israeli military officials told NPR they need "several more weeks" to complete war objectives. Netanyahu acknowledged Trump's diplomatic push but said Israel will protect its vital interests.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Mohammad Baqer Zolqadr appointed new head of Iran's Supreme National Security Council
outlets.summary: Iranian state TV leads with the appointment of hardline IRGC veteran Zolghadr as SNSC head, framing it as a show of strength amid ongoing US-Israeli aggression.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Iran foreign minister signaled readiness for deal in call with US envoys
outlets.summary: Iran International reports Araghchi privately told Witkoff and Kushner that Mojtaba Khamenei has approved deal talks, despite Iran's public denial of any negotiations.

outlets.name: IRNA
outlets.headline: Iran denies Trump's 'productive talks' claim — 'no direct or indirect contact' with US
outlets.summary: IRNA publishes Iran Foreign Ministry's categorical denial of Trump's Truth Social claim of productive negotiations, with FM Araghchi's statement that the claim is designed to 'mislead oil traders.'

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Araghchi secretly signals deal readiness while IRGC calls Trump "deceitful" — Iran's dual-track diplomacy exposed
outlets.summary: Iran International reports Araghchi privately told Witkoff and Kushner he has Mojtaba Khamenei's blessing for deal talks. Yet publicly, IRGC calls Trump a "deceitful president" and parliament speaker calls peace talk claims "fakenews." Iran's dual-track strategy: deny publicly, negotiate covertly.
